title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown acknowledged past criticisms about his left hand struggles, citing a significant left wrist injury in 2021.
Brown had a major surgery in 2021 and re-injured his left wrist in 2023, impacting his performance.
Since recovering, Brown improved his left-hand dribbling and is having a career-best season with 28.7 points per game.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo, former Red Sox play-by-play announcer, returned to Boston with the San Diego Padres for a game at Fenway Park.
Orsillo, working with the Padres since 2016, reflects on his NESN tenure and calls it his 'dream job'.
Orsillo reunited with former NESN colleagues and shared memories, receiving warm recognition from Boston fans.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Roman Anthony has mostly played as DH this season due to defensive issues noted by Red Sox manager Alex Cora.
Cora cited improvements needed in throwing accuracy but praised Anthony's performance despite his early-season struggles.
Anthony is batting .231 with a home run; Red Sox hold a 2-6 record ahead of Sunday's game against the Padres.

title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Connelly Early walked four and allowed three hits in four innings during Red Sox 3-2 loss to Padres.
Aroldis Chapman allowed the winning run in the ninth, despite Early's effort to limit damage early in the game.
Early's start was his second of the season, marking 39 strikeouts over his first six career MLB games.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 win over CF Montreal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution won 3-0 against CF Montreal, with goals from Luca Langoni, Mamadou Fofana, and Peyton Miller.
Head coach Marko Mitrović received a red card for leaving the technical area during the 34th minute of the match.
Revolution continues success with set pieces, as Fofana scores from a Langoni free kick, celebrating with set piece coach Ortí Esteban.

title: Here’s the latest on Red Sox’ Triston Casas, Johan Oviedo injuries
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Triston Casas's rib injury will allow him to resume hitting from a tee on Monday, per Red Sox manager Alex Cora.
Johan Oviedo will consult with a surgeon due to elbow strain and velocity drop, after March 30 pitching struggles.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval, recovering from injuries, are progressing toward minor league rehab outings.

title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, a 22-year-old cancer survivor from Dedham, will run the Boston Marathon for Mass General Hospital.
Diagnosed at 19 with aggressive thyroid cancer, Taylor credits MGH doctors with saving her life through specialized treatment.
Taylor views the marathon as a tribute to her doctors, AYA survivorship program friends, and patients facing cancer.

title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23 from South Boston, runs the 2026 Boston Marathon supporting Boston Medical Center's social workers.
Beattie aims to raise $10,000 for BMC's safety-net mission, highlighting social workers' vital role and thin resources.
Training includes events like HandleBar classes and Stats Bar fundraisers, emphasizing community's role in supporting BMC.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave, still seeking their first NWSL win.
Coach Filipa Patão emphasizes patience and better performance in the final third of the field.
Forward Amanda Gutierres, yet to score for Legacy FC, needs to focus on attacking closer to goal.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr., a suspended attorney from Melrose, was arraigned on embezzlement and larceny charges in Malden District Court.
Prosecutors allege Rappa misused hundreds of thousands from clients, practicing law despite suspensions in 2025 and 2026.
Rappa's bail was set at $2,500; he was ordered to surrender his passport and cease contact with witnesses.
